content.cn_text: 大家早上好，欢迎来到我们的户外生存节目。
content.en_text: Good morning everyone, and welcome to our outdoor survival programme.

content.cn_text: 如你所知，本周你将学习一些基本的信息和技能，你需要在户外独立照顾自己。
content.en_text: As you know, this week you'll be learning some of the basic information and skills you need to look after yourself independently in the outdoors.

content.cn_text: 头两天我们将在这里的教室里学习，然后我们将进行一次露营旅行，把你们学到的一些东西付诸实践。
content.en_text: These first two days we'll be based here in the classroom, and then we'll be taking a camping trip to put into practice some of the things you've learned.

content.cn_text: 我将从食物这个话题开始。
content.en_text: I'm going to start off with the topic of food.

content.cn_text: 首先，我将介绍两种方法，我们将在我们的营地实践，并利用自然资源:蒸汽坑和竹锅。
content.en_text: And to start with I'll describe just two methods which we'll be putting into practice at our camp, and which make use of natural resources:the steam pit and the bamboo pot.

content.cn_text: 我在这里有两张海报，让事情更清楚……我将从这里的蒸汽坑开始…
content.en_text: I've got two posters here to make things clearer ... And I'll start with the steam pit here ...

content.cn_text: 要做这个，你需要一些干树枝，一些草，一些松散的土和一些石头。这周只会有一些比赛!
content.en_text: To make this you'll need some dry sticks, some grass, some loose earth and some stones.And for this week only, some matches!

content.cn_text: 你要做的第一件事就是在你选择做饭的地方挖一个浅坑。大概25厘米深，30厘米宽。
content.en_text: The first thing you do is to dig a shallow pit in the place you've chosen to do your cooking.Let's say about twenty five centimetres deep, and thirty centimetres wide.

content.cn_text: 你的
content.en_text: Your

content.cn_text: 棍子必须比坑宽一点，因为你必须在坑的一端到另一端沿顶部放一排棍子。
content.en_text: sticks have to be a bit wider than the pit, because you have to put a line of them along the top from one end of the pit to the other.

content.cn_text: 在点燃这些之前，你拿一些大石头，把它们放在上面。
content.en_text: Before setting light to these you take some large stones and arrange them on top.

content.cn_text: 然后你开始生火，等待木头平台被烧透，石头掉进坑里。
content.en_text: Then you start the fire and wait till the wooden platform burns through and the stones fall into the pit.

content.cn_text: 这时，刷掉石头上的热灰——你可以用一把草——然后用另一根棍子把它插到坑的中心，石头之间。
content.en_text: At this point,brush away any pieces of hot ash from the stones - you can use a handful of grass -and then take another stick and push it down into the centre of the pit, between thestones.

content.cn_text: 然后用一层厚厚的草覆盖整个坑。
content.en_text: After that you cover the whole pit with a thick layer of grass.

content.cn_text: 然后你可以把食物放在上面。
content.en_text: And then you can put your food on it.

content.cn_text: 裹着更多的草，像包裹一样。
content.en_text: wrapped in more pieces of grass, like parcels.

content.cn_text: 最后，用泥土覆盖整个东西。
content.en_text: Finally, cover the whole thing with earth.

content.cn_text: 你得使劲拍，把坑封住。
content.en_text: You have to pat it firmly to seal the pit.

content.cn_text: 然后你要做的就是把棍子拿出来，在它留下的缺口里倒一点水。
content.en_text: Then all you have to do is take the stick out and pour a bit of water into the opening that it leaves.

content.cn_text: 你的食物煮熟大约需要四个小时，因为它在坑里产生的蒸汽中慢慢煮熟。
content.en_text: It should take about four hours for your food to cook, as it cooks slowly in the steam that's created inside the pit.

content.cn_text: 所以…简单但有效。
content.en_text: So ... simple but effective.

content.cn_text: 这周你们要练习的另一种方法是竹制烤炉。
content.en_text: The other method you're going to practise this week is the bamboo oven.

content.cn_text: 蒸汽坑在某些情况下是理想的，因为热量在地面以下，例如，如果有强风，你担心火灾会蔓延。
content.en_text: Now the steam pit is ideal in certain conditions because the heat is below ground level, for example, if there's a strong wind and you're afraid a fire might spread.

content.cn_text: 但如果明火是安全的，你可以用竹制烤炉。
content.en_text: But when it's safe to have an open fire you can use the bamboo oven method.

content.cn_text: 你会得到一段竹子，你可能知道它是中空的，由许多独立的部分组成，中间有一堵墙。
content.en_text: You get a length of bamboo, which as you probably know is hollow, and consists of a number of individual sections with a wall in-between.

content.cn_text: 你用一根锋利的棍子在每一面墙上打一个洞，把最后一面墙分开。
content.en_text: You use a sharp stick to make a hole in each of the dividing walls apart from the end one.

content.cn_text: 然后你把竹子靠在火上，用一根分叉的棍子支撑着竹子的顶部，底部放在地上。
content.en_text: Then you lean the bamboo over a fire, with the top propped up by a forked stick and the bottom sitting on the ground.

content.cn_text: 你在顶部倒入足够的水来填满底部，然后在底部点燃火来加热水。
content.en_text: You pour enough water in the top to fill the bottom section, and then light a fire underneath that section to heat the water.

content.cn_text: 然后你把食物放进顶部的部分，蒸汽通过你打的洞从竹子上冒出来，把它煮熟。
content.en_text: Then you put your food inside the top section, and the steam coming up the bamboo through the holes you made cooks it.

content.cn_text: 现在我要继续讲食物本身，谈谈一些你可能会烹饪的野生植物。
content.en_text: I'm going to move on now, to food itself, and talk about some of the wild plants you might cook.

content.cn_text: 我将从真菌开始，也就是蘑菇和毒菌。
content.en_text: I'm going to begin with fungi - that's mushrooms and toadstools.

content.cn_text: 我相信你会意识到其中一些是可食用的，它们很美味，但有些是剧毒的。
content.en_text: I'm sure you'll be aware that some of these are edible, and they're delicious, but some of them are highly poisonous.

content.cn_text: 不管它们是否有毒，你在野外发现的所有真菌都应该在食用前煮熟，因为这有助于破坏它们体内可能有轻微毒性的化合物。
content.en_text: Now whether they're poisonous or not, all fungi that you find in the wild should be cooked before eating, because that helps to destroy any compounds in them that might be mildly toxic.

content.cn_text: 但请注意，任何程度的烹饪都不会使有毒品种的食物更安全。
content.en_text: But be aware that any amount of cooking won't make poisonous varieties any safer to eat.

content.cn_text: 除非你能确定是一种真菌，否则永远不要吃。
content.en_text: Unless you can definitely identify a fungus you should never eat it.

content.cn_text: 不值得冒这个险。
content.en_text: It's not worth the risk.

content.cn_text: 你需要非常确定，因为一些有毒的真菌在外观上与某些可食用的品种非常相似。
content.en_text: And you need to be really sure, because some fungi that are poisonous are very similar in appearance to certain edible varieties.

content.cn_text: 他们很容易被误认为是彼此。
content.en_text: They can easily be mistaken for each other.

content.cn_text: 所以…说了这么多，新鲜采摘的真菌很美味，虽然它们只有中等营养，但它们确实含有人体所需的矿物质。
content.en_text: So ... having said all that, fungi are delicious when they're freshly picked, and although they are only moderately nutritious,they do contain minerals which the body needs.

content.cn_text: 现在我将继续讲叶植物，它们通常是……
content.en_text: I'll move on now to leafy plants, which are generally ...

groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
You will hear a trainer giving a talk to people who want to learno utdoor survival skills.
Complete the flow chart below.
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to
questions 11-16.

items.title: Arrange a row of  over the pit.
items.analysis: 根据原文描述，在制作蒸汽坑时，需要在坑的顶部放置一排木棍。文中明确提到"你要沿着坑的顶部从一端到另一端放置一排木棍"，这与题干中"Arrange a row of ___ over the pit"完全吻合。因此，正确答案是"sticks"（木棍）。

items.title: Place   on top.
items.analysis: 文中明确提到要在木棍上面放置大石头："Before setting light to these you take some large stones and arrange them on top"。这里的"on top"直接对应题干中的"Place on top"，而"stones"正是选项F中的内容。因此，F. stones是正确答案。

items.title: Remove
items.analysis: 文中明确提到要"brush away any pieces of hot ash"，即扫掉热灰。"brush away"是"remove"的同义词，而被移除的物品是"ash"（灰）。这与题目要求的"Remove"动作相符，因此正确答案是B. ash。

items.title: Cover the pit with
items.analysis: 根据原文描述，在准备蒸汽坑烹饪时，覆盖坑的材料是草。文中明确指出"After that you cover the whole pit with a thick layer of grass"，这直接对应了题干中的"Cover the pit"。因此，正确答案是D. grass。这个步骤是在放入食物之前进行的，是烹饪过程中的一个关键步骤。

items.title: Place wrapped food on top, and cover it with
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C. earth（土），因为在描述制作蒸汽坑的最后一步时，讲者明确指出要用土覆盖整个坑。这是整个过程的最后一步，用"Finally"这个词作为信号。使用土覆盖是为了密封坑洞，确保蒸汽不会逃逸，从而实现有效的烹饪。

items.title: Remove the stick and put into the hole.
items.analysis: 文中明确指出在取出木棍后，需要往留下的开口处倒入一些水。这个动作直接对应题干中描述的"Remove the stick and put into the hole"。因此，正确答案是G（water），因为水是被倒入孔中的物质。

groups.title: Which TWO characteristics apply to the bamboo oven?

collect_options.title: Which TWO characteristics apply to the bamboo oven?

list.title: B
list.content: The fire is lit below the bottom end of the bamboo.

list.title: C
list.content: The bamboo is cut into equal lengths.

list.title: D
list.content: The oven hangs from a stick.

list.title: E
list.content: It cooks food by steaming it.

items.analysis: 根据原文描述，竹炉（bamboo oven）有两个主要特征：一是在竹子底部下方点火（选项B），二是通过蒸汽烹饪食物（选项E）。文中明确提到"light a fire underneath that section"（在该部分下方点火）和"the steam coming up the bamboo through the holes you made cooks it"（蒸汽通过你在竹子上开的孔上升，将食物蒸熟），这两点直接对应了选项B和E的描述。因此，B和E是正确答案。

根据原文描述，竹炉（bamboo oven）有两个主要特征：一是在竹子底部下方点火（选项B），二是通过蒸汽烹饪食物（选项E）。文中明确提到"light a fire underneath that section"（在该部分下方点火）和"the steam coming up the bamboo through the holes you made cooks it"（蒸汽通过你在竹子上开的孔上升，将食物蒸熟），这两点直接对应了选项B和E的描述。因此，B和E是正确答案。

groups.title: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about eating wild fungi?

collect_options.title: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give about eating wild fungi?

list.title: A
list.content: Cooking doesn't make poisonous fungi edible.

list.title: B
list.content: Edible wild fungi can be eaten without cooking.

list.title: D
list.content: Some edible fungi look very similar to poisonous varieties.

list.title: E
list.content: Fungi which cannot be identified should only be eaten in small quantities.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A和D。根据原文，讲话者给出了两个关于食用野生菌的重要建议：首先，烹饪不能使有毒的菌类变得安全可食用；其次，有些有毒菌类和可食用菌类外观非常相似，容易混淆。这两点分别对应了选项A和D的内容，因此它们是正确答案。
这道题的正确答案是A和D。根据原文，讲话者给出了两个关于食用野生菌的重要建议：首先，烹饪不能使有毒的菌类变得安全可食用；其次，有些有毒菌类和可食用菌类外观非常相似，容易混淆。这两点分别对应了选项A和D的内容，因此它们是正确答案。
